methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Fuse Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by JC Btaiche, currently valued at $200M+ post-money and having raised $52M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 11 comparable peers: 3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method, grounded in the company's $200M last post-money valuation. Applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for the Series A to subsequent-stage transition yields an implied valuation of $400M. Peer-median triangulation was not viable; while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier produced fewer than 3 datapoints, rendering narrow-set comparables insufficiently robust.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a median of $400M. This range reflects conservative (low) and optimistic (high) interpretations of the stage-progression multiple against the current post-money base. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to the broader peer set, technical risk in the company's core technology, and the limited public-market data on nuclear frontier-tech valuations. The single-method foundation warrants sensitivity analysis should additional peer financings or market data emerge.
